rag_rules: Address AGREE syntax errors like missing 'annex' keywords or unresolved identifiers.
Focus repairs within the existing AGREE annex structure; avoid architectural changes.
generic_rules: Validator diagnostics indicate issues with AGREE contract syntax or unresolved references.
Repairs should prioritize fixing type mismatches and ensuring valid AGREE scalar types (int, real, bool).
Use only visible AADL features or helper declarations within the same annex to avoid resolution errors.
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: AGREE contracts reside within `annex agree { ... };` blocks.
Helper declarations (eq) should align types with the expressions they define.
anti_patterns: Do not introduce new AADL features or package dependencies to resolve AGREE errors.
Avoid attempting to repair contracts with fundamentally invalid syntax; consider removing the clause.
irrelevant_topics: AADL structural extraction (component types, implementations, features)
AGREE contract generation (assume/guarantee syntax, assign, fusion)
